description: A registry that does NOT advertise "did:key" in supported_did_methods MUST reject a did:key publish with key_resolution_failed (permanent, HTTP 400) — the code choice pinned by RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.1 and RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.4. Not key_resolution_unreachable (nothing transient — retrying against this registry can never succeed without a capability change), and not unsupported_algorithm (ed25519 itself is supported; the DID method is what is not).

input.scenario: The sig-003 golden publish request (valid did:key identity, valid signature) submitted to a registry whose capabilities document advertises only did:web

expected.behavior: The request is cryptographically flawless — rejection is purely capability-driven and permanent for this registry. The registry SHOULD include details naming the unsupported method (e.g. details.did_method = "did:key") so the producer can route to a did:key-capable registry. A 0.1.0 registry (which by definition does not advertise did:key) behaves identically under its existing rules: agent_id is not did:web → rejected (pub-008 semantics).
